title: Home
text:
Hi, I’m Lucas Gates, a Data Science student at the University of Missouri (Mizzou).
I’m interested in data science + software engineering, especially projects involving: - Python / R - data pipelines - analytics and visualization
You can find my work on GitHub: https://github.com/Lucas-Gates

title: Internship Skill Analysis
text:
Repo: https://github.com/Lucas-Gates/internship_skill_analysis

Summary
This project analyzes early-career job postings to identify in-demand skills and statistically meaningful technical stacks.


Key Results

Python and SQL are the most common technical skills
Distinct stacks appear:

BI: SQL Server + Power BI
ML: PyTorch + TensorFlow
Data Engineering: Hadoop + Spark




Example Visualizations

title: Missouri Budget Graph Maker
text:
Problem
Budget/expenditure data is often published as large spreadsheets that are difficult to explore quickly without writing code or manually filtering.


What I built
A lightweight desktop GUI (Tkinter) that loads Missouri state expenditure data and lets a user generate charts with a few clicks.

control_panel.py implements a Tkinter control panel UI
graph.py loads a CSV (for example: 2024_State_Expenditures.csv) and generates Matplotlib bar charts
main.py launches the app



Key features

GUI-driven filtering/sorting
Fast category exploration
Automatically generates readable bar charts



Skills demonstrated

Python desktop UI (Tkinter)
Data wrangling in pandas
Visualization in Matplotlib
Project structure + reproducible run entrypoint



How to run (typical)
From the project root (where requirements are installed): ```bash python src/python/main.py

title: Missouri Budget Graph Maker
text:
Budget/expenditure data is often published as large spreadsheets that are difficult to explore quickly without writing code or manually filtering.



A lightweight desktop GUI (Tkinter) that loads Missouri state expenditure data and lets a user generate charts with a few clicks.

control_panel.py implements a Tkinter control panel UI
graph.py loads a CSV (for example: 2024_State_Expenditures.csv) and generates Matplotlib bar charts
main.py launches the app





GUI-driven filtering/sorting
Fast category exploration
Automatically generates readable bar charts





Python desktop UI (Tkinter)
Data wrangling in pandas
Visualization in Matplotlib
Project structure + reproducible run entrypoint
